project.name: aethon
project.description: Aethon is a developer-facing desktop application: a thin Tauri 2 + React 19 shell that embeds the pi coding agent as a bun subprocess and renders its output as live, interactive UI via the A2UI protocol. The agent drives the interface (layout-as-payload) instead of a fixed IDE, while the Rust shell owns OS boundaries — agent process supervision, PTY shells, git/GitHub status, voice capture, auto-update, and LAN peer discovery.

nodes.name: Agent Orchestration & Bridge
nodes.summary: Owns the lifecycle of the embedded pi coding agent: a global bridge plus one worker bridge per non-default tab, spawned as bun subprocesses and supervised by the Rust shell. Forwards chat messages and A2UI events over JSON-lines stdio, multiplexes tabs over a single pipe, idle-retires workers, and survives hot reloads without aborting in-flight prompts.

businessRules: Tab-scoped messages with a non-default tabId route to that tab's worker; everything else goes to the global bridge
Workers spawn lazily on first write, respawn when their cwd changes, and idle-retire after the configured TTL (15 min default)
The global bridge is the sole owner of the frontend extension surface; worker registry mutations are origin-gated by originTabId
Hot reload drains in-flight prompts via a reload sentinel instead of SIGKILL

nodes.name: Send a chat message to the agent
nodes.summary: User submits a prompt; the Rust shell routes it to the global or per-tab bridge, the bridge runs the pi session, and streamed assistant output flows back as agent-response events that mutate the UI.

nodes.name: Forward message into bridge stdin
nodes.summary: send_message forwards the chat message and attachments from the frontend into the agent bridge's stdin, choosing global vs worker by tabId.

nodes.name: Spawn or route to the bridge worker
nodes.summary: The supervisor lazily spawns the bun bridge on first write (or routes to an existing worker), launching the child with user/worker env and devshell-resolved environment.

nodes.name: Dispatch the inbound JSON-lines message
nodes.summary: The bridge's central dispatcher reads stdin and routes each inbound message to its matching handler, carrying the tabId through AsyncLocalStorage.

nodes.name: Run the pi session for the tab
nodes.summary: Tab lifecycle resolves the tab's cwd, lazily builds its pi session with the full tool registry, and streams assistant text/thinking/tool events into session messages.

nodes.name: Emit agent-response events
nodes.summary: Reader threads on the child's stdout emit an agent-response Tauri event per line (UTF-8-boundary safe), which the frontend consumes and applies to the state store.

nodes.name: Idle-retire a background worker
nodes.summary: A background sweep task retires worker bridges that have been idle past the configured TTL and reaps orphaned meta-less children, reconciling against the frontend's live tab set.

nodes.name: Track per-worker readiness and idle time
nodes.summary: The supervisor state machine tracks per-worker readiness, last-activity, and wedged-prompt protection in the AgentProcesses managed state.

nodes.name: Sweep workers past the TTL
nodes.summary: The background idle-sweep task retires idle workers past the configured TTL and reaps orphaned children, unless a wedged prompt is still being protected.

nodes.name: Purge mutation routes on retire
nodes.summary: Mutation acknowledgement routes for the retired worker are purged so pending mutation ids can no longer resolve against a dead process.

nodes.name: Hot-reload the agent bridge
nodes.summary: An extension file change triggers a graceful bridge reload: the watcher writes a reload_request to stdin, the bridge drains active prompts and emits a sentinel, and the supervisor respawns on the next IPC call without aborting the user's turn.

nodes.name: Request a graceful reload
nodes.summary: The Rust file-watcher debounce worker writes a reload_request message to the child's stdin instead of SIGKILL when extension files change.

nodes.name: Drain in-flight prompts
nodes.summary: The bridge dispatcher drains active prompt sessions for each tab so the user's in-flight LLM turn is not aborted by the reload.

nodes.name: Emit the reload-done sentinel and exit
nodes.summary: The bridge emits a _reload_done sentinel on stdout and exits; the supervisor's reader peeks for it, emits agent-reloaded, and respawns on the next IPC call.

nodes.name: A2UI Rendering, Layout & State
nodes.summary: The frontend rendering surface where the agent decides what the user sees. The default UI is a data payload (workstation.a2ui.json), fed through the A2UIRenderer that turns layout payloads into a live React tree of primitives plus extension components. All app state lives in one object addressed by JSON Pointer, with optimistic $ref updates on user input.

businessRules: The default layout is data, not hardcoded React, so the agent can mutate the entire chrome at runtime
All app state lives in one object addressed by JSON Pointer; reads use {"$ref": "/path"} bindings
The renderer applies optimistic writes back to the $ref path for change/submit events before the backend confirms
20 built-in primitives are non-overridable; all other chrome composites come from the ExtensionRegistry

nodes.name: Boot the frontend and paint the default layout
nodes.summary: The React app applies the saved theme, mounts the thin App shell, bootstraps the single state store from the default-layout payload, and renders the workstation chrome through the A2UIRenderer.

nodes.name: Apply boot theme early
nodes.summary: The application entry point applies the saved boot theme as early as possible, before the React app mounts, to avoid a flash of unthemed chrome.

nodes.name: Compose the App shell from hooks
nodes.summary: App.tsx composes ~40 useX() hooks, bootstraps the single state store, wires OS-edge listeners and native window sync, and renders the workstation layout.

nodes.name: Load the layout-as-payload default
nodes.summary: The default-layout extension assembles the slot catalogue and registers all chrome components, panels, themes, and keybindings behind workstation.a2ui.json.

nodes.name: Render the A2UI tree
nodes.summary: The A2UIRenderer turns the layout payload into a live React tree, wiring the 20 built-in primitives plus extension components and handling for-each templating.

nodes.name: Apply an agent UI mutation
nodes.summary: Agent output arrives as a batched agent-response payload; each bridge message is dispatched to its handler, origin-gated, and applied to the single state store, which re-renders the affected slice of the React tree.

nodes.name: Pump batched agent-response messages
nodes.summary: useBridgeMessages wires the agent-response Tauri event into a batched payload pump and dispatches each message to its registered handler.

nodes.name: Origin-gate registry mutations
nodes.summary: Registry-replacing messages are rejected unless their origin tab is in the active workspace bucket, stopping a background worker from clobbering active components.

nodes.name: Write into the JSON-Pointer state store
nodes.summary: The JSON-Pointer toolkit resolves and sets the target path in the single app-state store with structural-sharing clones.

nodes.name: Re-resolve $ref bindings and re-render
nodes.summary: Data-binding re-resolves each component's $ref JSON-Pointer props into concrete values and the renderer repaints the affected subtree.

nodes.name: Route a user interaction event
nodes.summary: When the user clicks or types, the central event dispatcher resolves the A2UI event through shell-consent prefixes, extension routes, then id/type-keyed built-in handlers, applying an optimistic state update and optionally forwarding to the bridge.

nodes.name: Apply optimistic $ref update
nodes.summary: For change/submit events on $ref inputs the renderer applies an optimistic write back to the bound path so the UI feels instant.

nodes.name: Dispatch through the precedence layers
nodes.summary: The central dispatcher resolves the event through shell-consent prefixes, extension-registered routes, then id/type-keyed built-in handlers in that order.

nodes.name: Forward to the bridge or mark handled
nodes.summary: A handler returning true marks the event handled and suppresses the default dispatch_a2ui_event forward to the backend; returning false forwards it to the agent.

nodes.name: Interactive Shells & Devshell
nodes.summary: Beyond the agent, the Rust shell hosts interactive PTY terminal tabs. Shells opened inside a flake project are auto-wrapped with the resolved Nix devshell environment, which feeds both the PTY tabs and the agent's bash tool from one source of truth. A per-tab ShareMode is the security boundary that gates whether the agent may read or write a shell.

businessRules: ShareMode is the per-tab opt-in security boundary; agents only see shared shells via list()
Writes pop an Allow/Deny notification in read-write mode; read-write-trusted bypasses the prompt
Devshell env is applied after TERM/COLORTERM and before per-tab overrides so explicit env keeps winning
A cold devshell cache spawns the shell unwrapped while a background resolver warms it

nodes.name: Open a PTY shell tab
nodes.summary: The user opens a terminal sub-tab; the Rust shell spawns a portable-pty process, applies the resolved Nix devshell env, and streams output back to the frontend as shell-output events.

nodes.name: Resolve the Nix devshell env
nodes.summary: The devshell resolver runs nix print-dev-env / direnv exec env / nix-shell with a timeout and parses the null-separated env output, cached by lockfile fingerprint.

nodes.name: Spawn the portable-pty shell
nodes.summary: shell_open spawns a new interactive PTY shell tab, applying the resolved devshell env after TERM/COLORTERM and before per-tab overrides.

nodes.name: Stream PTY output to the frontend
nodes.summary: The reader emits shell-output events as the PTY produces bytes and shell-exit when the process ends, preserving UTF-8 boundaries across reads.

nodes.name: Append into bounded scrollback
nodes.summary: Output is appended into a bounded ring-buffer scrollback store that tracks oldest/total offsets and supports reads from an arbitrary offset.

nodes.name: Grant the agent access to a shell
nodes.summary: An agent requests to read or write a shared shell; the ShareMode boundary decides — surfacing only opted-in shells, prompting Allow/Deny on writes, and bypassing the prompt only in trusted mode.

nodes.name: Check the per-tab ShareMode
nodes.summary: The ShareMode/ShareState transition logic enforces the per-tab sharing boundary (none / read-only / read-write / read-write-trusted) before any access is allowed.

nodes.name: Prompt for write consent
nodes.summary: A write in read-write mode pops an Allow/Deny notification, rounding through the mutation-ack channel; read-write-trusted bypasses the prompt.

nodes.name: Apply the read or write
nodes.summary: Once consented, the bridge surface (aethon.shells read/write) reads scrollback or forwards keystrokes into the PTY writer for that tab.

nodes.name: Projects, Workspaces & Git Integration
nodes.summary: Manages the project/workspace tree (a project has one or more workspaces — the main checkout or a git worktree), each running independently with its own tabs, sessions, and git state. An always-on VCS surface consolidates working-tree changes, branch ahead/behind, PR state, and CI rollup into one slice, fed by tiered status polling and TTL-cached gh data.

businessRules: A project has one or more workspaces; existing tabs keep the cwd they were created with when the active project switches
The project list is MRU, capped at 16, persisted with schema migration on read
Git-status polling is tiered: hot (active workspace), warm (recent roots), cold (other projects)
Use gh issue list length for the open-issue count, not open_issues_count which counts PRs plus issues

nodes.name: Switch the active workspace
nodes.summary: Selecting a workspace in the sidebar paints warm from in-memory caches, mirrors the active workspace id to state so the file tree and new tabs follow, and reconciles git status in the background.

nodes.name: Reconcile workspace records
nodes.summary: Workspace domain logic reconciles in-memory workspace records against the git worktree listing, managing pending states and ordering.

nodes.name: Warm-paint from the VCS slice cache
nodes.summary: The /vcs slice paints from the in-memory LRU plus disk-backed VCS slice cache for an instant warm switch while data reconciles.

nodes.name: Restore the workspace's tab bucket
nodes.summary: Tabs bucket per workspace, so switching restores the active workspace's tabs while existing tabs keep the cwd they were created with.

nodes.name: Schedule a tiered status refresh
nodes.summary: The tiered poll scheduler tracks a warm-root MRU set and computes which workspace roots are due for a git-status refresh at warm vs cold cadence.

nodes.name: Refresh the always-on VCS surface
nodes.summary: On a poll tick the VCS hook calls git status, per-file status, gh branch status, and gh checks (all best-effort), then consolidates branch ahead/behind, PR state, and CI rollup into the single /vcs slice.

nodes.name: Query git working-tree status
nodes.summary: The git status module produces a branch/ahead/behind/dirty summary and per-file porcelain-z classification against the active workspace root.

nodes.name: Fetch branch PR status
nodes.summary: The gh branch-status cache fetches PR state for the current branch over the gh CLI with positive/negative TTLs and in-flight dedupe.

nodes.name: Roll up CI check runs
nodes.summary: The gh checks cache fetches the CI check-run rollup for the branch, mapping individual check conclusions to a single tone.

nodes.name: Consolidate into the /vcs slice
nodes.summary: useVcsStatus consolidates working-tree changes, ahead/behind, PR state, and CI rollup into one /vcs state slice read by both the header cluster and the source-control panel.

nodes.name: Create a git worktree workspace
nodes.summary: The user adds a workspace; the Rust worktree commands create a new git worktree on a branch, and the workspace store reconciles it into the in-memory list and the sidebar tree.

nodes.name: Add the git worktree
nodes.summary: The worktree lifecycle commands list/add/remove worktrees on a branch, with orphan/markerless cleanup and prune support.

nodes.name: Persist into the projects store
nodes.summary: The projects store persists the MRU project list and per-project workspaces to disk with schema migration on read.

nodes.name: Reconcile into the sidebar tree
nodes.summary: The workspace logic reconciles the new worktree into the in-memory workspace records so it appears nested under its project in the host->project->workspace tree.

nodes.name: Voice-to-Text Input
nodes.summary: Composer dictation living Rust-side. A common engine trait fronts multiple providers — a local Whisper model (candle-transformers), native OS recognizers (macOS SFSpeechRecognizer, Windows SAPI), and an experimental LFM2-Audio path — all consuming the same captured PCM buffer from the cpal recorder so the orchestrator needs no per-OS branching.

businessRules: All providers consume the same CapturedAudio PCM buffer, so the core holds a single engine trait with no per-OS branching
Whisper model weights are downloaded on demand via prepare and removable
The active provider is selectable and enableable; capture meters levels in real time

nodes.name: Dictate a transcript
nodes.summary: The user starts dictation; the cpal recorder captures PCM while metering levels, then on stop the selected provider transcribes the buffer and returns text into the composer.

nodes.name: Start recording on the provider
nodes.summary: start_recording begins capture on the selected provider, dispatching to the platform, Whisper/Distil, or LFM2 path.

nodes.name: Capture PCM and meter levels
nodes.summary: The cpal recorder captures PCM into a RecordingSession, meters levels via a spawned emitter task, and normalizes/resamples the interleaved samples.

nodes.name: Stop and transcribe
nodes.summary: stop_and_transcribe stops the active recording and returns the transcript from the selected provider's engine.

nodes.name: Deliver text to the composer
nodes.summary: The transcript is surfaced through the voice IPC command back to the frontend composer for the user to send.

nodes.name: Prepare a voice model
nodes.summary: Before first use of a Whisper provider the model weights are downloaded on demand into the local cache, and can be removed to reclaim space.

nodes.name: Select and enable a provider
nodes.summary: The voice IPC surface lists, selects, and enables a transcription provider before any recording can begin.

nodes.name: Download the model weights
nodes.summary: Whisper model weights are downloaded on demand into the on-disk cache when the provider is prepared.

nodes.name: Load the inference engine
nodes.summary: The voice core loads the active provider engine so it is ready to transcribe captured PCM through the common engine trait.

nodes.name: Platform Services: Update, Discovery & Auth
nodes.summary: Self-contained desktop subsystems that round out the shell. Channel-aware auto-updates with a pre-install backup and post-launch rollback timer; an mDNS discovery scaffold that advertises and browses for peer Aethon instances on the LAN; and per-tab auth profiles letting different agent tabs authenticate as different accounts.

businessRules: Auto-update copies the running .app to a versioned backup before install; a bounded probation timer rolls back on failed boot
The mDNS advertiser is gated on [server] enabled, but the HTTP server and browser always run so discovery stays read-only and useful
Discovery is explicit scaffolding with no auth and no TLS — not to be leaned on for trusted IPC
Profile ids are sanitized before touching disk; the active profile selects which model registry a tab sees

nodes.name: Auto-update with rollback protection
nodes.summary: A background poll discovers an update on the selected channel, stashes it, and on install backs up the current .app before applying; the post-launch probation monitor arms a rollback timer that the frontend cancels on a healthy first paint.

nodes.name: Check for an update on the channel
nodes.summary: check_for_updates_with_channel discovers nightly/stable endpoints via the GitHub API and stashes the pending Update in managed state.

nodes.name: Back up the running app
nodes.summary: install_pending_update copies the current .app to a versioned backup dir inside spawn_blocking before applying the update.

nodes.name: Install the stashed update
nodes.summary: The stashed pending Update is installed, after which the app relaunches into a probation window.

nodes.name: Arm the rollback timer
nodes.summary: The probation monitor tracks boot stages and arms an attempt-bounded rollback timer that spawns the rollback helper on timeout.

nodes.name: Confirm a healthy boot
nodes.summary: useUpdater calls boot_stage and boot_ok on first paint to cancel the rollback timer once the new version launches cleanly.

nodes.name: Discover LAN peers over mDNS
nodes.summary: The discovery daemon advertises the local Aethon over _aethon._tcp.local. with its HTTP port, browses for other instances, and emits host-discovered / host-removed events the frontend surfaces.

nodes.name: Bind the discovery HTTP server
nodes.summary: An axum HTTP server binds an OS-chosen port exposing GET /health and /status for mDNS-discovered peers (no auth, scaffold).

nodes.name: Advertise and browse over mDNS
nodes.summary: The mDNS advertiser announces _aethon._tcp.local. with the HTTP port in TXT while the browser scans for peers, emitting host-discovered / host-removed.

nodes.name: Surface discovered peers
nodes.summary: The host command reports local host identity and the discovered peers are surfaced to the frontend for display.

nodes.name: Log in with an auth profile
nodes.summary: A user adds or switches a per-tab account; the auth-profiles manager runs the OAuth or API-key flow, persists sanitized credentials, and applies the active profile so that tab's agent authenticates as the chosen account.

nodes.name: Send the auth-profile command
nodes.summary: Frontend command helpers resolve switch targets and send auth-profile bridge commands to the agent for the active tab.

nodes.name: Run the OAuth or API-key flow
nodes.summary: The auth-profiles manager orchestrates credential persistence and the OAuth/API-key login flow, streaming login events so the OAuth challenge surfaces in the UI.

nodes.name: Persist the sanitized profile
nodes.summary: The store derives on-disk paths, sanitizes the profile id before touching disk, and saves the profile metadata and credentials.

nodes.name: Apply the per-tab model registry
nodes.summary: The active profile selects which model registry a tab sees, so per-tab pi service registries authenticate as the chosen account.

nodes.name: Extensions & Runtime API
nodes.summary: The override surface that lets extensions reshape Aethon. A public window.aethon / globalThis.aethon runtime API lets tools and extensions register components, themes, keybindings, and layouts, and mutate the UI. Extensions load from a directory or npm-style package, and the ExtensionRegistry maps component/template types to React implementations so chrome composites can be swapped at runtime.

businessRules: Chrome composites must be keyed by type so registerComponent and renamed instances stay routable; the 20 primitives can't be overridden
Layout ids match /^[A-Za-z][\w-]*$/ and 'workstation' is reserved
Extension themes reject reserved ids and invalid CSS variable keys
The global bridge owns the frontend extension surface; worker registry mutations are origin-stamped and gated

nodes.name: Load extensions at bridge startup
nodes.summary: On bridge startup the extension loader discovers pi-installed extensions and npm-style packages from the user directory, imports candidate files, and registers their component/theme/keybinding exports before the default tab's session prompt is composed.

nodes.name: Discover installed extensions
nodes.summary: The extension loader discovers pi-installed Aethon extensions and persisted session tabs from the user directory.

nodes.name: Import and register exports
nodes.summary: The directory loader imports candidate files, registers their exports, and handles project-scoped naming for the active cwd.

nodes.name: Load npm-style packages
nodes.summary: The package loader discovers and loads npm-style Aethon extension packages from their manifest, registering backend exports and forwarding UI registrations.

nodes.name: Compose the system prompt with extensions
nodes.summary: main.ts builds AethonAgentState and loads extensions before the default tab so its session prompt and runtime snapshot see them.

nodes.name: Register a component via the runtime API
nodes.summary: An extension or pi tool calls the runtime API to register a custom component, theme, or layout; the registration is stamped with its origin tab, gated by the frontend, and applied to the ExtensionRegistry so chrome composites swap at runtime.

nodes.name: Call the runtime registration API
nodes.summary: globalThis.aethon assembles the full runtime API (layout, canvas, components, themes, keybindings) that tools and extensions call to mutate the UI.

nodes.name: Stamp the origin tab
nodes.summary: Registry-replacing bridge messages are stamped with the worker's originTabId so background-workspace workers can't clobber the active workspace's surface.

nodes.name: Round-trip the mutation ack
nodes.summary: mutation-ack mints a tracked mutation id with a timeout, gates the send on frontend readiness, and resolves when the frontend confirms or rejects.

nodes.name: Apply to the ExtensionRegistry
nodes.summary: The ExtensionRegistry maps the component/template type to its React implementation, swapping the chrome composite at runtime for all instances keyed by type.

nodes.name: Register and activate a layout
nodes.summary: Through window.aethon a caller registers a new layout payload (validated against the canonical slot contract and id pattern) and activates it, replacing the workstation chrome that the A2UIRenderer paints.

nodes.name: Register the layout payload
nodes.summary: windowApi builds the public window.aethon API including registerLayout/activateLayout, enforcing the id pattern and the reserved 'workstation' id.

nodes.name: Validate the slot coverage
nodes.summary: The slot catalogue exposes coverage inspection to validate that a layout satisfies the canonical slot contract (header, sidebar, canvas, composer, terminal, status).

nodes.name: Activate and repaint
nodes.summary: Activating the layout swaps the active payload, and the A2UIRenderer repaints the entire chrome from the new data document.

edges.description: The bridge streams agent-response events that the frontend applies to the single state store, re-rendering the A2UI tree.
User events that aren't handled locally are forwarded to the bridge via dispatch_a2ui_event for the agent to act on.
The global bridge is the sole owner of the frontend extension surface; worker registry mutations are origin-gated.
Each workspace pins its own cwd; workers respawn when their cwd changes and idle-retire per workspace.
Devshell resolution feeds both PTY shells and the agent's bash tool from one source of truth; ShareMode gates agent shell access.
Shells opened inside a flake project are auto-wrapped with that project's resolved Nix devshell environment.
Dictated transcripts are delivered back into the composer surface rendered by the A2UI frontend.
Auth profiles select which model registry each agent tab authenticates against.
The update banner and probation report render as chrome above the A2UI layout.
The active workspace id is mirrored to state so the sidebar tree, file tree, and new tabs follow the selection.
Extensions register components, themes, and layouts into the ExtensionRegistry that the A2UIRenderer paints, overriding chrome composites.
